producer_note: Iconic Right Bank estate in Pomerol owned by the Moueix family. 11.4ha vineyard producing ~30,000 bottles/year, since 2010 100% Merlot from iron-rich clay terroir.
Seventh-generation Gevrey-Chambertin estate (Trapet Pere et Fils), 18.5ha biodynamic (Demeter-certified since 2009). Owns Grand Crus Chambertin, Latricieres-Chambertin and Chapelle-Chambertin.
Founded 1900 by Georges de Latour in Rutherford AVA, Napa Valley. Famed for Georges de Latour Private Reserve Cabernet (first vintage 1936). Now owned by Treasury Wine Estates (since 2016).
First Growth (1855) located in Pessac-Leognan, the oldest of Bordeaux's first growths dating to 1525. Owned by Domaine Clarence Dillon family.
First Growth (1855) located in commune of Margaux, Medoc. 87ha entitled to Margaux AOC, planted 75% Cabernet Sauvignon, 20% Merlot, with Cabernet Franc and Petit Verdot.
Cult micro-estate in Pomerol founded 1979 by Jacques Thienpont. Tiny 2.7ha vineyard planted to ~100% Merlot, producing only 600-700 cases per year.
Family roots in Puligny-Montrachet since 1717; 24ha entirely Chardonnay, including Batard-Montrachet and Chevalier-Montrachet Grand Crus. Biodynamic since 1996.
5.5ha estate in Vosne-Romanee, run by Maxime Cheurlin since 2010. Holdings include Clos de Vougeot Grand Cru, Vosne-Romanee 1er Cru Beaux Monts and Chaumes.
Family-owned negociant founded 1797, based in Beaune. Owns 119 acres across 24 Cote d'Or appellations and buys grapes from 130 different appellations; largest Grand Cru holdings in Cote d'Or.
Tiny 4.5ha Pomerol estate dating to 1872, owned by Sylvie and Jacques Guinaudeau. Planted 60% Merlot and 40% Cabernet Franc, ~1,000 cases of grand vin annually.
Saint-Emilion estate established 1832, owned by LVMH and Frere family. 39ha vineyard planted 57% Cabernet Franc, 40% Merlot. Held Premier Grand Cru Classe A status until 2021.
Family estate based in Vosne-Romanee since 1620 (Mongeard-Mugneret name from 1920). 30ha across 37 appellations including Richebourg, Grands Echezeaux, Echezeaux and Clos de Vougeot.
Second Growth (1855) in Saint-Estephe, owned by Olivier and Martin Bouygues since 2006. 168 acres planted 65% Cabernet Sauvignon, 25% Merlot, 10% Cabernet Franc.
Third Growth (1855) located in Cantenac sector of Margaux. 66ha biodynamic since 2013, planted 47% Merlot, 47% Cabernet Sauvignon, 6% Petit Verdot. Managed by Thomas Duroux.
Founded 1996 in Chassagne-Montrachet, 6.3ha mainly Chardonnay; first organic certification in Chassagne (2012). Holdings also in Puligny-Montrachet, Meursault, Pommard, Beaune.
First Growth (1855) at southeastern tip of Pauillac. Owned by Francois Pinault since 1993. Grand vin typically 75% Cabernet Sauvignon, 20% Merlot. ~18,000 cases annually.
Founded 1971 by Giovanni Neri in Montalcino, run by son Giacomo and grandsons. 75ha across six estates producing acclaimed Brunello including White Label, Tenuta Nuova and Cerretalto.
Super Tuscan estate established 1981 by Lodovico Antinori in Bolgheri DOC. 97ha planted with Bordeaux varieties; owned by Frescobaldi since 2005. Produces Ornellaia, Masseto, Le Serre Nuove.
Estate in Chassagne-Montrachet founded 2004 after split from Colin-Deleger family domaine. ~5ha (after 2021 division), Chardonnay and Pinot Noir; grand cru Chevalier-Montrachet.
Born 1963 from Louis Gros inheritance, based in Vosne-Romanee. 19ha including Grand Crus Clos de Vougeot, Echezeaux, Grands Echezeaux and Richebourg. Run by Bernard Gros's children.
Premier Cru Superieur from Sauternes, owned by LVMH (since 1996). 113ha planted 80% Semillon, 20% Sauvignon Blanc; ~65,000 bottles per year of botrytized sweet wine.
Founded 1994 by Bernard Dugat and Jocelyne Py in Gevrey-Chambertin. 15ha, organic since 1999 (certified 2003). Grand Crus include Chambertin, Mazis-Chambertin, Charmes-Chambertin.
First Growth (1855) in Pauillac, owned by Rothschild family since 1868. 107ha vineyard planted ~70% Cabernet Sauvignon, 25% Merlot. Run by Saskia de Rothschild since 2018.
Family estate in Gevrey-Chambertin since 1956, now run by Laurence and her children Clemence and Arnaud. 16ha across 100+ plots from village to Grands Crus Chambertin and Mazis-Chambertin.
Joint venture launched 1979 by Robert Mondavi and Baron Philippe de Rothschild, located in Oakville AVA, Napa Valley. Single Bordeaux-style red blend from estate vineyards in To Kalon area.
Fifth Growth (1855) in Pauillac owned by the Cazes family since 1939. 90ha gravel ridge planted 73% Cabernet Sauvignon, 15% Merlot, 10% Cabernet Franc, 2% Petit Verdot.
Legendary Amarone producer founded 1924 in Negrar, Valpolicella Classica, run by Quintarelli family. 13ha, ~60,000 bottles per year of Amarone, Recioto and Valpolicella Classico Superiore.
Family domaine in Gevrey-Chambertin since 1955, now run by Bertrand and Laetitia Dugat. 6ha including Lavaux Saint-Jacques, Charmes-Chambertin, Chapelle-Chambertin and Griottes-Chambertin.
Historic Barolo producer dating to 1890, based in La Morra near Barolo. Owns large parcel of the iconic Cannubi cru. Also produces Barbera, Langhe Nebbiolo and Barolo Chinato.
Family-owned negociant since 1831, based in Beaune. Owns 100+ha across six estates spanning Chablis, Cote de Nuits, Cote de Beaune, Cote Chalonnaise and Beaujolais.
Premier Grand Cru Classe A in Saint-Emilion since 2012, owned by Gerard Perse since 1998. 50ha planted 50% Merlot, 30% Cabernet Franc, 20% Cabernet Sauvignon.
Estate established early 1980s in Chassagne-Montrachet by Richard Fontaine and Laurence Gagnard. 12ha, 10 Premier Crus and Grand Crus Criots-Batard-Montrachet, Batard-Montrachet, Montrachet.
Brunello estate founded 1970 in Canalicchio district north of Montalcino, run by Giancarlo Pacenti since 1988. Produces Brunello di Montalcino, Vecchie Vigne and PS (Pelagrilli).
Historic Saint-Emilion estate owned by the Vauthier family since 1690. 7ha planted 50% Cabernet Franc, 50% Merlot on limestone. ~2,000 cases annually of grand vin and Chapelle d'Ausone.
Founded 1985 in Rutherford Bench, Napa Valley. 51 acres of organic, solar-powered estate vineyards planted to Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot, Chardonnay and Sangiovese.
Premier Grand Cru Classe A (since 2022) in north-west Saint-Emilion. 41ha planted 35% Cabernet Sauvignon, 35% Cabernet Franc, 30% Merlot on Gunzian gravel.
Rothschild Lafite group; portfolio includes Chateau Lafite Rothschild (Pauillac), Duhart Milon (Pauillac), Rieussec (Sauternes), L'Evangile (Pomerol), plus Bordeaux/Haut-Medoc holdings.
Second Growth (1855) in Pauillac owned by AXA Millesimes since 1987. 73ha planted 65% Cabernet Sauvignon, 30% Merlot, 3% Cabernet Franc, 2% Petit Verdot. Managed by Christian Seely.
Family-owned Langhe winery founded 1937 by Riccardo Ceretto. 25ha across four estates producing both Barolo (Bricco Rocche, Castiglione Falletto) and Barbaresco (Bricco Asili). Organic since 2015.
Second Growth (1855) in Saint-Julien, owned by Borie family since 1941, run by Bruno Borie. 50ha gravel beds planted 65% Cabernet Sauvignon, 25% Merlot. Converting to organic from 2018.
Cult Napa estate founded 1984 by Bill Harlan in western hills of Oakville. 240-acre property with ~40 acres planted to Bordeaux varieties (70% Cabernet Sauvignon). First commercial vintage 1990.
Brunello estate established 1981 by Pierluigi Talenti in southern Montalcino's Orcia valley, now run by grandson Riccardo. 40ha estate, ~half planted to Sangiovese for Brunello and Rosso.

Estate based in Meursault since 1868, co-owned by Labruyere family. 21ha of holdings spanning nine grand crus and 14 premier crus across Cote de Nuits and Cote de Beaune (Chambertin, Musigny, Montrachet).
Founded 1977 by Rivetti family in Castagnole delle Lanze (Asti). Produces Moscato d'Asti, Barbera, and three Barbarescos (Gallina, Starderi, Valeirano), plus Barolo Campe in Grinzane Cavour.
Boutique Barossa Valley label producing Shiraz from South Australia's Barossa region; limited public information beyond regional sourcing.
Negociant arm of Domaine Claude Dugat in Gevrey-Chambertin, run by Bertrand and Laetitia Dugat. Produces Gevrey-Chambertin and 1er Cru bottlings sourced from the Gevrey appellation.

Founded 1972 by John Shafer in Stags Leap District, Napa Valley. 250 acres mainly in Stags Leap; flagship Hillside Select Cabernet first released 1981. John Shafer helped establish the SLD AVA in 1985.
Pomerol estate dating to 1687, owned by Domaines Barons de Rothschild (Lafite) since 1990. 12ha next to Petrus and La Conseillante, planted 80% Merlot, 20% Cabernet Franc.
Family domaine based in Morey-Saint-Denis, run by Cyprien Arlaud since 1997. ~16ha across Morey-Saint-Denis, Chambolle-Musigny, Gevrey-Chambertin; organic 2004, biodynamic certified 2014.

Founded 1961 by Joe and Alice Heitz, based in St. Helena. 425 acres of organic vineyards across six Napa appellations, including iconic Martha's Vineyard (Oakville). Owned by Lawrence family since 2018.
Family producer founded 1956 in Calamandrana, Piedmont. Vineyards across Barolo (Cerequio, Cannubi), Barbaresco, Nizza/Asti (La Court Barbera) and Gavi DOCG.
Historic St. Helena winery, the first built in Napa after Prohibition (1933); owned by Schlatter family. Bordeaux-style wines (flagship Profile blend) sourced from multiple Napa AVAs.
Boutique Brunello estate adjoining Biondi Santi, run by Caroline Pobitzer and Jan Hendrik Erbach. 6ha biodynamic since 2003, producing Brunello and Rosso di Montalcino from Sangiovese.
Founded 1976 by Dan and Margaret Duckhorn in St. Helena. Bordeaux-style Napa Valley wines (renowned Three Palms Merlot) sourced from estate vineyards across multiple Napa sub-AVAs.
Pessac-Leognan Cru Classe (Graves 1953), sister property to Haut-Brion (Domaine Clarence Dillon). 21ha planted 48% Cabernet Sauvignon, 45% Merlot, 7% Cabernet Franc.
Estate founded 1976 in Gaiole in Chianti, run by Lorenza Sebasti and winemaker Marco Pallanti. High-elevation Chianti Classico (Gaiole UGA), best known for Gran Selezione 'San Lorenzo'.

Domaine founded 1960 by Joseph Roty in Gevrey-Chambertin, now run by his sons Pierre-Jean and Philippe. 12ha mainly in Gevrey and Marsannay including Charmes-Chambertin Grand Cru with 1881-planted vines.
Second Growth (1855) in Saint-Estephe founded 1791 by Louis Gaspard d'Estournel; owned by Michel Reybier since 2000. 100ha planted 56% Cabernet Sauvignon, 40% Merlot. Fully organic from 2024.
Family-owned Napa winery founded 1973 by Jack and Dolores Cakebread, located in Rutherford. Best known for estate Chardonnay, Sauvignon Blanc and Cabernet Sauvignon.
Second Growth (1855) in Saint-Julien, owned by the Delon family since the late 19th century. 97ha planted 65% Cabernet Sauvignon, 19% Merlot. Second wine Clos du Marquis since 1902.
Cult Amarone producer in Cellore d'Illasi, Val d'Illasi (Verona), led by Romano Dal Forno since the early 1980s. 34ha producing Amarone and Valpolicella Superiore 'Monte Lodoletta'.
Small Burgundy producer based in Vosne-Romanee. Wines sourced across Aloxe-Corton, Beaune, Pommard, Gevrey-Chambertin, Nuits-Saint-Georges and Vosne-Romanee appellations.
Family Valpolicella producer with roots to 1630, based in Pedemonte (Valpolicella Classica). 46ha hillside vineyards; flagship Amarone Capitel Monte Olmi pioneered single-vineyard Amarone in 1960s.
Fifth Growth (1855) in Pauillac, owned by Baron Philippe de Rothschild family since 1970. 41ha next to Mouton Rothschild and Lafite, planted 50% Cabernet Sauvignon, 37% Merlot.
Historic Barolo estate founded 1858 by Conte Mirafiore (son of King Vittorio Emanuele II) in Serralunga d'Alba. Largest contiguous estate in Langhe (~250 acres); fully certified organic since 2018.
Founded 1982 by Sir Peter Michael in Knights Valley AVA on slopes of Mt. St. Helena. ~149 acres planted; produces single-vineyard Cabernet, Chardonnay, Pinot Noir and Sauvignon Blanc.
Third Growth (1855) and northernmost classified growth in Medoc, located in Saint-Estephe. 55ha walled vineyard; owned since 2012 by Suravenir Assurances with JP Moueix as minority partner.
Pomerol plateau estate owned by the Laborde family since 1998, run by Ronan Laborde from 2004. 11ha planted 75% Merlot, 25% Cabernet Sauvignon. One of the oldest Pomerol vineyards (1785).
Cru Classe of Graves located in Pessac, owned by Bernard Magrez since 1985. 66ha planted vines; oldest wine estate in Bordeaux, with first recorded harvest in 1252.
Burgundy estate founded 1885 in Volnay, based in Meursault since 2005. 19ha specialized in Puligny-Montrachet (incl. Clos de la Mouchere monopole) and Volnay, plus Savigny and Beaune.
Modern Chinese estate founded 2017 by ex-Changyu winemaker Zhang Yanzhi in Qingtongxia, Ningxia. Manages 2,133ha; signature variety is Cabernet Gernischt (Carmenere clone).
Founded 1973 by Hubert Lamy in Saint-Aubin, Cote de Beaune; led today by son Olivier Lamy. 18.5ha (80% Chardonnay) including holdings in Puligny, Chassagne, Santenay and tiny Criots-Batard plot.

Family-owned estate established by Novak family 1982 in St. Helena, Napa Valley (vineyard first planted 1882). 33-acre Cabernet vineyard plus Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot and Sauvignon Blanc; certified organic since 1992.
Family-owned producer founded 1881 in Alba, run by 5th generation Federica Boffa. 70ha of estate vineyards across Barolo and Barbaresco DOCG zones; also Barbera, Dolcetto, Moscato d'Asti.
Family-owned Napa Valley winery founded 1992 by Randy and Debbie Lewis on Silverado Trail. Cabernet Sauvignon and Chardonnay sourced from multiple Napa Valley vineyards.
Fifth Growth (1855) in Pauillac, owned and run by the Borie family (Francois-Xavier Borie). 55ha planted 75% Cabernet Sauvignon, 20% Merlot, 5% Cabernet Franc on deep gravel.
Pernand-Vergelesses estate exclusively producing two Grand Crus on the hill of Corton: 9.5ha Corton-Charlemagne (Chardonnay) and 1.5ha Corton (Pinot Noir). Owned by Stan Kroenke since 2017.
Brunello producer founded 1972 in eastern Montalcino; acquired by Roberto Giannelli in 2003. 22ha near Biondi Santi's Il Greppo, producing Brunello (incl. cru Le Lucere) and Rosso di Montalcino.
Family-owned Napa Green-certified producer founded by Dwight Bonewell, with vineyards focused in Rutherford. Produces approachable Cabernet Sauvignon, Chardonnay and Chenin Blanc.
Premier Grand Cru Classe B in Saint-Emilion, owned by Chanel (Wertheimer family) since 1996. 21.5ha on the limestone plateau, planted 60% Merlot, 40% Cabernet Franc.
Negociant founded 1859 in Nuits-Saint-Georges. Cultivates 230ha (Ecocert organic) across Chablis, Cote de Nuits, Cote de Beaune, Maconnais, Beaujolais and Cote Chalonnaise.
Major negociant-eleveur based in Beaune since 1859. 528 acres of vineyards including ~280 acres of Premier and Grand Cru in the Cote d'Or, plus Beaujolais and Chablis holdings.
Mariani family estate founded 1978 in Montalcino. 2,830ha contiguous property (about one-third planted to vine) producing Brunello di Montalcino plus Super Tuscan and other Tuscan wines.
Family producer founded 1960s by Sergio Zenato. 95ha across two areas: Lugana on south shore of Lake Garda (Turbiana whites) and Valpolicella Classica in Sant'Ambrogio (Amarone, Ripassa).
Family producer founded 1978 in La Morra, Langhe. 156ha including five Barolo crus (Cerequio, Bussia Bofani, Brunate, Briccolina, Boscareto) plus Barbaresco, Barbera, Dolcetto, Moscato d'Asti.

Volnay-based estate run by Benoit Landanger (succeeding Patrick Landanger, owner since 1997). Three Volnay 1er Cru monopoles plus 7 Grand Crus and 11 Premier Crus across Cote d'Or; biodynamic since 2012.
Pomerol estate dating to 1761, owned by Jean-Pierre Moueix family since 1953. 7.2ha on western Pomerol plateau planted 90% Merlot, 10% Cabernet Franc; aged in 50% new oak.
Historic Langhe producer (Poderi e Cantine Oddero) based in Santa Maria di La Morra, roots to late 18th century. 35ha across Barolo, Barbaresco, Barbera d'Asti and Moscato; certified organic 2017.

Brunello estate established 1975 in Castelnuovo dell'Abate, southeast Montalcino. Five single-vineyard sites (incl. Schiena d'Asino, Loreto) producing Brunello and Rosso di Montalcino. CEO Andrea Machetti.
Fifth Growth (1855) in Pauillac, owned by Tesseron family since 1975. 81ha planted 62% Cabernet Sauvignon, 32% Merlot, with biodynamic management since 2004 (full conversion).
Small family winery in Rabaja, Barbaresco. ~20 acres of estate vines producing three Barbarescos (entry-level, single-cru Rabaja, and Riserva in top vintages) aged in used Slavonian oak casks.
Founded 1998 by acclaimed Napa winemaker Bob Foley (formerly of Markham and Pride Mountain). Estate on Howell Mountain plus sourced Cabernet from Mount Veeder, Napa Valley and Carneros.
Pomerol estate dating to 1756, owned by Nicolas family since 1871. 13ha bordering L'Evangile, Vieux Chateau Certan and Cheval Blanc; planted 80% Merlot, 20% Cabernet Franc.
Founded 2006 by Robin and Michelle Baggett, located in Rutherford. Wines blended from estate vineyards plus sites across nearly all 16 Napa sub-AVAs (valley floor, hillside and mountain).

Tenute Cisa Asinari dei Marchesi di Gresy, family estate east of Alba; family acquired Martinenga vineyard in 1797 and began estate bottling in 1973. ~45ha; sole owner of Barbaresco's iconic Martinenga monopole.
Second Growth (1855) in Margaux owned by Lawrence Wine Estates since 2022. 130ha vineyard (one of largest in Medoc); plantings unusually Merlot-heavy (~50%) with Cabernet Sauvignon and Petit Verdot.

Historic Rioja Alta bodega founded 1852 by Don Luciano de Murrieta; first Rioja exporter. 300ha Finca Ygay estate in southern Rioja Alta, owned by the Cebrian family since 1983. Flagship Castillo Ygay Gran Reserva.

Gevrey-Chambertin estate established 1974 by Alain Burguet, taken over by sons Jean-Luc and Eric in 2011. 10ha farmed organically since the 1980s; flagship 'Mes Favorites' Vieilles Vignes village cuvee.
